title: Multimodal imaging pipeline for molecular and cellular characterization of Liver tissue - HuBMAP TTD-Columbia/PSU
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7i45v","text":"We have developed a workflow to assess the spatial organization of liver tissue with regard to its cellular and metabolic heterogeneity at tissue and single cell level resolution - by performing multimodal imaging on consecutive tissue sections using H\u0026E staining, DESI,  (H2O)n-GCIB-SIMS, C60-SIMS, and RNAscope.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":274,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":277,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5l85","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4l9ik","text":"This document provides the collection of protocols within the multimodal imaging workflow implemented by Columbia University/Pennsylvania State University Transformative Technology Development Center as a part of Human Biomolecular Atlas Program (HuBMAP, NIH).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare tissue sections for multimodal imaging on appropriate substrates. dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.e6nvwjmjzlmk/v1
H&E staining is performed using standard protocol.
DESI-IMS is performed as described here. dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ewov1nze7gr2/v1
(H 2 O) n -GCIB-SIMS-IMS is performed as described here. dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.81wgbyynovpk/v1
C60-SIMS for multiplexed antibody staining is performed as described here. dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.b5qmq5u6
RNAScope imaging is performed as described here. dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.kqdg3p3e7l25/v1
creator: Presha Rajbhandari
